sn45.42-47:1.5: Addhāna (“course of time”) is a word for “transmigration” (saṁsāra), usually expressed as the “past (course)” and “future (course)” (SN 12.20:5.2). It is rooted in the metaphor of a journey, especially the long, dark road of the stars, sun and moon in the sky (eg. Rig Veda 1.113.3, 6.12.3, 6.29.2). Contrast the “first beginning” and “final end” (MN 79:7.5).
